level_signals: Compensation range $215K-$300K base consistent with Staff-level IC
Expected to own full product surface end-to-end, not just features

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time owning a user-facing product end-to-end (not just features within someone else's product)
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time owning a user-facing product end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building 0-to-1 products or leading major product pivots where the problem definition was as important as the solution
Strong product instincts for UX: you care about how things feel to users, not just whether they technically work
Comfort with technical depth. You don't need to write code, but you should be able to engage with engineers on system design, architecture tradeoffs, and what's actually hard
Experience shipping to enterprise customers with real governance, compliance, or regulatory constraints
Ability to operate in ambiguity, form strong opinions quickly, and update them when the evidence changes

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bonus: experience with AI/ML products, legal technology, or professional services

scope.surface: Core surface within Harvey's Agent platform — covering how lawyers interact with agents, how agents get created and discovered, or the underlying platform infrastructure
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS — AI agents for legal and professional services
primary_metrics_inferred: Product usage depth (substantive work completed via agents)
Enterprise customer deployment success (law firms and in-house legal teams)
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end product lifecycle for a core surface within Harvey's Agent platform, from problem definition through launch and iteration
Define and drive the product vision for how lawyers interact with AI agents, grounded in real user workflows and firm-level deployment constraints
Work directly with engineering, design, and applied legal research to ship high-quality product on an aggressive timeline
Navigate enterprise complexity: ethical walls, audit trails, governance, multi-stakeholder approval flows
Partner with GTM to shape how the product gets positioned, demoed, and adopted by the world's largest law firms and in-house legal teams
Make hard prioritization calls with imperfect data in a fast-moving competitive landscape

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($215K-$300K base)
Clear product surface articulated — not a vague 'AI platform' role
0-to-1 opportunity with real ownership at Staff level
Strong product-market fit signals: 1000+ customers in 60+ countries
Explicit acknowledgment that enterprise governance constraints are core product work, not afterthoughts
red_flags: No location or remote policy stated — candidates cannot determine work arrangement requirements
Very wide comp band ($215K-$300K) may indicate significant uncertainty in leveling or location-based adjustment
Exact scope of role ('exact scope will be shaped by your strengths') is intentionally vague — ownership could shift post-hire

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff', comp range, and years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer mentions)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Depending on your location' language in privacy notice section)
ambiguities: Location/work arrangement is entirely absent from the JD body — no city, remote policy, or hybrid language. Set to 'unclear'.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Set to 'unclear'.
The compensation note 'Depending on your location' appears in a privacy notice context but may also signal location-based pay differentials.
